spec_match.rationale: Excellent conceptual simulation of the three-actor push synchronization architecture, detailing the exact mechanics of the pre-138 key-rotation bug.

interactive.rationale: Highly interactive, allowing users to toggle between pre- and post-138 event behaviors and trace delivery status.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Beautifully aligns with the core problem statement of silent push endpoint expirations.

edge_cases.rationale: No feature detection on load, and a styling issue makes the text inside fresh/stale boxes invisible by setting background and color to the same variable.

references.rationale: Provides clear spec links and ChromeStatus entry references.

design_system.rationale: Uses repo layouts, but the color-contrast bug in .stale/.fresh overrides creates an unreadable UI.

openQuestions.title: Fix text invisibility contrast bug in stale/fresh endpoint boxes
openQuestions.detail: In server-sync-flow/index.html, the classes `.endpoint.stale` and `.endpoint.fresh` define both background and color using the same CSS variable (e.g., `var(--accent-rose)`). This makes the text inside these blocks completely invisible. Fix them to use high-contrast pairings (e.g., background: `color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent-rose) 20%, transparent); color: var(--text-black)`).

openQuestions.title: Add browser capability checks for PushManager
openQuestions.detail: Add a lightweight browser check on load to verify if PushManager and ServiceWorkers are supported natively in the visitor's browser.

summary: A highly educational and robustly modeled simulation showing how Push key rotations are synchronized across Service Workers and Backends. However, the active CSS sets the background and text colors to identical values in status boxes, rendering the endpoint text invisible.
